title: Tour Guide

rationales.taskAutomation: Self-guided AI audio-tour apps now deliver competent historical narration, but live storytelling, group management, and adapting to questions stay human.
rationales.toolMaturity: GPS-triggered AI audio-guide apps are mature and widely available as a direct substitute for a basic guided walk.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of self-guided AI tour apps among independent travelers is growing steadily but remains an early-adopter behavior overall.
rationales.agenticExposure: Route scripting and narration generation are agent-ownable; live group management and real-time Q&A are not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard historical narration for common routes
Basic FAQ handling about a site or itinerary
tasksGrowing: Group management and pacing for varied traveler needs
Handling unscripted questions and local color
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-7011, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Group-management and safety judgment on the move

insight: An AI audio guide can narrate a landmark competently for a fraction of the cost. What it can't do is read a group's energy, take a real question off-script, or turn a rained-out afternoon into a good story anyway.
